methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $3.1B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.8B - $312.5B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Palantir
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $312.5B

private.name: Crusoe Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $10B+

private.name: SandboxAQ
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $5.75B

private.name: Ayar Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $3.75B

data.narrative:
Lambda, a Series E AI & Software company that has raised $3.15B to date, operates within a competitive frontier-tech landscape. The peer set comprises nine comparable entities—one public (Palantir) and eight private firms including Crusoe Energy, SandboxAQ, Ayar Labs, and Waymo—all at Series E stage or equivalent maturity.

Two valuation methods anchor this analysis. Stage-progression methodology, applying a 1.4x multiplier to total capital raised as a baseline proxy, implies a $4.4B valuation. Peer-median analysis across the four most relevant same-stage, same-sector comparables yields a $7.9B implied value, though the peer range spans $3.8B (Ayar Labs) to $312.5B (Palantir), reflecting significant variance in business model and scale. The peer median of $7.9B provides the higher bound given market precedent for late-stage AI firms.

These methods triangulate to a valuation range of $3.7B to $9.1B, with a median of $6.1B. The lower bound reflects capital deployed; the upper bound reflects peer multiples. Key uncertainties include Lambda's revenue profile and unit economics relative to the comparison set, particularly given Palantir's outsized valuation, and the degree to which private-market conditions in AI have compressed or expanded relative to the peer cohort. Absent disclosure of Lambda's financial performance, the $6.1B midpoint represents the most defensible central estimate.
